description: Rewrite the mARCH TUI from Rich/prompt_toolkit to Textual, providing a reactive widget tree with modals, streaming, themes, and proper async support

userStories.title: Textual App Scaffold + Core Layout
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH, I want the TUI to run as a Textual App with a structured layout so that I have a stable foundation for all UI widgets.
userStories.notes: This is the foundation story. Keep old TUI code intact — wire new app as the default entry point but don't delete existing files.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py with MarchApp(App) class using Textual CSS for vertical layout: Header | ConversationArea | InputBar | Footer
Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/__init__.py as the widget package with re-exports
Mount placeholder Static widgets for each layout zone so the app renders immediately
Handle on_key for Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D to exit cleanly via self.exit()
Update src/mARCH/cli/cli.py to call MarchApp().run() instead of the old TUI entry point, keeping old code importable
acceptanceCriteria: MarchApp class exists in src/mARCH/ui/tui_app.py and subclasses textual.app.App
App launches with 4 visible layout zones (header, conversation, input, footer)
Ctrl+C and Ctrl+D exit the app cleanly without traceback
src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/__init__.py exists and is importable
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Header & Banner Widget
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the project name, version, and mode indicator in a header bar so that I always know what mode I'm in.
userStories.notes: Use importlib.metadata to get version. ASCII mascot is optional decoration — hide it on narrow terminals.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/header.py with HeaderWidget(Static) class
Display project name 'mARCH', version from importlib.metadata, and current mode string
Include a simple ASCII mascot as static text; hide it when terminal width < 80 columns using Textual CSS media queries or reactive sizing
Wire into tui_app.py by replacing the header placeholder widget in compose()
acceptanceCriteria: HeaderWidget renders project name 'mARCH' and version string
Mode indicator displays current mode text (e.g., INTERACTIVE)
Widget degrades gracefully when terminal width is less than 80 columns
Unit test verifies header content includes 'mARCH'
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Message Display Widget
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see conversation messages rendered with role labels, markdown, and syntax-highlighted code blocks so that the conversation is readable.
userStories.notes: Use Textual's built-in Markdown widget for rich content rendering. Role colors should come from the theme system once TUI-009 lands, but hardcode for now.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/conversation.py with ConversationView(VerticalScroll) as the scrollable message container
Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/message.py with MessageWidget(Static) for individual messages with role label and content
Support roles USER, ASSISTANT, SYSTEM, TOOL — each with a distinct label and color
Use Textual Markdown widget or Rich markup for markdown rendering including fenced code blocks with syntax highlighting
Auto-scroll to bottom on new message mount; show placeholder text when conversation is empty
acceptanceCriteria: ConversationView renders a list of MessageWidget instances
Each message displays role label (USER/ASSISTANT/SYSTEM/TOOL) with distinct color
Code blocks render with syntax highlighting
View auto-scrolls to the newest message when a new one is appended
Empty conversation shows a placeholder message rather than blank space
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Input Widget + Mode Cycling
userStories.description: As a user, I want an input bar with mode cycling so that I can switch between INTERACTIVE, PLAN, AUTOPILOT, and SHELL modes without leaving the keyboard.
userStories.notes: Match existing repl.py Shift+Tab cycling behavior. Don't delete repl.py — mark it deprecated.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/input_bar.py with InputBar(Widget) containing a Textual Input child widget and a mode label
Mode indicator shows current mode with color: INTERACTIVE(cyan), PLAN(yellow), AUTOPILOT(green), SHELL(red)
Bind Shift+Tab (backtab) to cycle modes in order: INTERACTIVE → PLAN → AUTOPILOT → SHELL → INTERACTIVE
Enter submits input text and clears field; Ctrl+C interrupts; Ctrl+N toggles multiline
Wire into tui_app.py replacing the input placeholder; add deprecation comment to src/mARCH/cli/repl.py
acceptanceCriteria: InputBar widget renders with mode label and text input field
Shift+Tab cycles modes in order: INTERACTIVE → PLAN → AUTOPILOT → SHELL → INTERACTIVE
Enter key submits the input text and clears the field
Mode indicator color changes per mode (cyan/yellow/green/red)
Unit test verifies mode cycling logic returns correct next mode
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Streaming Response Display
userStories.description: As a user, I want AI responses to stream token-by-token into the conversation view so that I see output in real time without flicker.
userStories.notes: This is the most technically challenging story — async streaming into a reactive widget must be flicker-free. Use Textual Worker pattern.
implementationNotes: Add append_chunk(text: str) method to MessageWidget in src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/message.py that reactively updates content using Textual reactive or call_from_thread
Update ConversationView in src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/conversation.py to create a placeholder ASSISTANT MessageWidget when streaming starts
Use Textual Worker pattern in tui_app.py for the async AI call + streaming loop that feeds chunks to append_chunk
Update src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py to provide a streaming callback hook compatible with the new TUI
Ensure no cursor artifacts or flicker by batching updates and using Textual's built-in refresh mechanism
acceptanceCriteria: append_chunk method exists on MessageWidget and updates displayed content reactively
Streaming tokens appear incrementally in the conversation view without full re-render
Worker pattern properly handles async streaming lifecycle (start, stream, complete, error)
No visible flicker or cursor artifacts during rapid streaming updates
Unit test verifies append_chunk accumulates text correctly across multiple calls
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Tool Call Approval Modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want a modal dialog for tool call approvals so that I can review and approve or deny tool executions with keyboard shortcuts.
userStories.notes: This modal blocks the main app until the user responds. Use Textual's push_screen/dismiss pattern.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/tool_modal.py with ToolModal(ModalScreen) class
Display tool name, description, and formatted arguments in a bordered panel
Bind keyboard shortcuts: y=yes_once, a=always, n=deny, Esc=deny — call self.dismiss(result) for each
Support arrow up/down navigation between options and number shortcuts 1/2/3/4 as alternative selection
Integrate in tui_app.py via self.push_screen(ToolModal(tool_info), callback) pattern
acceptanceCriteria: ToolModal subclasses textual.screen.ModalScreen
Modal displays tool name, description, and arguments in a readable layout
Keyboard shortcut 'y' dismisses with 'yes_once' result
Keyboard shortcut 'a' dismisses with 'always' result
Keyboard shortcut 'n' or Esc dismisses with 'deny' result
Unit test verifies dismiss values for each keyboard shortcut
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Status Indicators + Animated Spinners
userStories.description: As a user, I want animated spinners and status indicators so that I know when the system is processing and can see success, error, or warning states.
userStories.notes: Braille spinner is the default. Status bar replaces the footer placeholder.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/spinners.py with SpinnerWidget(Static) that uses set_interval for animation at ~100ms
Spinner frames: braille pattern ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏ cycling through on each timer tick
Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/status_bar.py with StatusBar(Static) showing operation text and status icon
Status icons: ✓ (success/green), ✗ (error/red), ⚠ (warning/yellow), ● (info/blue)
Wire status bar into tui_app.py footer zone, replacing the footer placeholder
acceptanceCriteria: SpinnerWidget animates through braille frames on a timer interval
StatusBar displays current operation text with the appropriate status icon
Status icons map correctly: success=✓, error=✗, warning=⚠, info=●
Spinner stops and clears when the associated operation completes
Unit test verifies spinner frame cycling returns correct sequence
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Plan Mode Display Modal
userStories.description: As a user, I want a modal for viewing and acting on plans so that I can review plan summaries and choose an execution action.
userStories.notes: This replaces the current plan_display.py approval flow. Keep old code until this modal is proven.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/tui_widgets/plan_modal.py with PlanModal(ModalScreen) class
Display plan summary rendered as markdown and a task list using Textual Markdown widget
Bind keyboard shortcuts: e=exit_only, i=interactive, a=autopilot, f=autopilot_fleet — call self.dismiss(action) for each
Return selected action string via dismiss() to the calling app callback
Replace current plan_display.py approval flow by wiring PlanModal into tui_app.py
acceptanceCriteria: PlanModal subclasses textual.screen.ModalScreen
Modal renders plan summary as formatted markdown
Keyboard shortcut 'e' dismisses with 'exit_only'
Keyboard shortcut 'i' dismisses with 'interactive'
Keyboard shortcut 'a' dismisses with 'autopilot'
Keyboard shortcut 'f' dismisses with 'autopilot_fleet'
Unit test verifies dismiss values for each action shortcut
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Theme System + Color Constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a centralized theme and color system so that all widgets use consistent brand colors and support future light/dark mode toggling.
userStories.notes: This is a pure data/utility story with no UI side effects. Other widgets adopt it once it lands.
implementationNotes: Create src/mARCH/ui/theme.py with a Theme dataclass containing color fields: brand, text, background, accent, border, statusSuccess, statusError, statusWarning, statusInfo, and per-mode colors
Create src/mARCH/ui/colors.py with named color constants as strings (hex or named) for brand, selected, each status type, and each mode (cyan/yellow/green/red)
Provide get_theme(dark: bool = True) function returning the active Theme instance (dark mode default, light mode available)
Wire brand color into header, borders, and mode indicator across widgets that already exist
acceptanceCriteria: Theme dataclass exists in src/mARCH/ui/theme.py with documented color fields
src/mARCH/ui/colors.py exports named color constants for brand, status, and mode colors
get_theme() returns a valid Theme instance with all required color fields populated
Dark mode is the default; get_theme(dark=False) returns a light mode variant
Unit test verifies get_theme() returns expected color values for both modes
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures
